prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (H2C=O) computed with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges scheme: Mulliken (PySCF). Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). N−1 cation: charge +1, mult 2; N+1 anion: charge −1, mult 2 (auto-derived). Geometry: input .xyz as supplied (not re-optimized at this level).

Fukui indices per atom:
  Atom 1 (O):  f+ = 0.46814, f- = 0.46742, f0 = 0.46778, dual = +0.00071
  Atom 2 (C):  f+ = 0.70839, f- = 0.11244, f0 = 0.41042, dual = +0.59595
  Atom 3 (H):  f+ = -0.08827, f- = 0.21008, f0 = 0.06090, dual = -0.29835
  Atom 4 (H):  f+ = -0.08826, f- = 0.21006, f0 = 0.06090, dual = -0.29832

Most electrophilic site: C (atom 2), f+ = 0.70839130 — headline value reported.
Most nucleophilic site: O (atom 1), f- = 0.46742464.

Negative Mulliken Fukui values on H atoms are a known basis-set polarization artifact of the finite-difference N±1 scheme and do not imply unphysical electron density.

Integrity: status=ok, trustworthy=true. All checks passed: sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000, schema_header ok. No warnings reported by the engine.
